config.note: isolated CUDA-graph M=1 micro-profiling of the real bf16 draft io_projection (kanna #264 bucket: pre_projection [256,5120] + post_projection [2560,256], the residual-stream in/out GEMVs -- NOT q/k/v/o) vs its HBM-bandwidth roofline; large embed_tokens GEMV as the empirical BW anchor. No serve change, no HF Job, no submission. Greedy+PPL pinned (nothing edited).

io_projection_components.name: pre_projection
io_projection_components.role: backbone->draft input projection (run first, before layer 0)

io_projection_components.name: post_projection
io_projection_components.role: draft->backbone output projection (run last, after model.norm)

levers.name: L0_interkernel_scheduling
levers.note: erase the ~60ns/node GPU-side scheduling between the pre and post launches. Does NOT touch either GEMV body (both intrinsic-M=1). Sub-0.1us -> immaterial. The two GEMVs are non-adjacent (opposite ends of the stack) so they cannot be fused into one.

levers.name: L_bandwidth_roofline_UNREACHABLE
levers.note: the analytic 600 GB/s floor; PHYSICALLY UNREACHABLE at M=1 (single-warp GEMV under-saturates HBM, ~31-81% of peak, Chen 2605.30571). Needs M>=16 BATCHING, not kernel surgery -> NOT a fusion lever, a different (tree/verify-shape) axis.

verdict.composition_overcredit_caveat: any projected_tps_gain_pct is a COMPOSITION projection (cheaper draft step -> TPS via official=K_cal*(E[T]/step)*tau), NOT a measured wall-clock gain: the model-forward (~1.2182ms step) is a fraction of the ~8ms conc=1 wall step, so the composition may OVER-CREDIT draft-step cuts. CONTINGENT on stark #273's warm wall-clock verdict. Moot here: recoverable=0 -> projected gain 0.0%.
verdict.fp_reassociation_flag: N/A for io_projection: the term is two NON-ADJACENT pure-linear GEMVs (pre at the start, post at the end of the pass), so there is no in-term fusion that reassociates a bf16 reduction order. (Contrast the MLP, where a split-K megakernel WOULD reassociate the down reduction -- not bit-identical, cf. lawine #246 -- and must be E[T]/PPL re-verified.) Any future adjacent-op fold (e.g. an RMSNorm into a GEMV epilogue/prologue) is elementwise on the post-reduction registers -> lossless -> E[T]+PPL unchanged, still propose-only safe.
verdict.verdict_line: INTRINSIC-M=1, NULL: the draft io_projection measures 14.0us at M=1 (pre 8.7us + post 5.6us) vs a 6.6us bandwidth floor -> overhead ratio 2.14x, but that is only 47% of peak HBM BW -- a single-warp M=1 GEMV UNDER-SATURATES HBM (Chen 2605.30571), so the gap is the INTRINSIC-M=1 memory-latency penalty, NOT recoverable slack (the large 128 MiB reference GEMV hits 81% of peak, IS bandwidth-bound, anchoring the model). UNLIKE the MLP (whose +4.4% came from its SEPARATE GeluAndMul companion kernel), io_projection is 2 PURE GEMVs at opposite ends of the stack with NO companion to fold and NO fuseable pair -> recoverable ~= 0 -> projected_tps_gain_pct = 0.00% off 481.53. Completes the draft decomposition: MLP+attn+io = 93.3us = 95% of the 98us GEMV chain, all intrinsic-M=1.
verdict.handoff_line: the draft io_projection is 14.0us (14% of the 101.2us/pass; pre 8.7us + post 5.6us), at 47% peak HBM BW so INTRINSIC-M=1 like the MLP GEMVs (the 128 MiB reference GEMV hits 81% = bandwidth-bound, anchoring the 600 GB/s model), meaning a greedy-safe fusion/launch-erasure does NOT net any TPS off 481.53 (it is 2 pure GEMVs at opposite ends of the stack with NO separable companion kernel -- unlike the MLP's GeluAndMul -- and the two cannot be fused), completing the draft decomposition (MLP+attn+io = 93.3us = 95% of the 98us GEMV chain) so the bf16 draft floor is ALL intrinsic-M=1 and the only draft lever is fewer passes (adaptive-K, ONEGRAPH-blocked) or M>=16 batching -- NOT cheaper draft passes. Hand-offs: wirbel #270 (attention, the OTHER GEMV term; together we complete the pass), denken #271 (g_d/overhead), fern #274 (portfolio: io_projection contributes 0 to the step budget), stark #273 (the wall-clock arbiter -- moot here, gain is 0).
